page: Google Cloud Platform n2-highmem-8 is a memory-optimized virtual machine instance. It features 8 vCPUs mapped to 4 physical cores of an Intel Xeon processor running at 2.8 GHz on the x86_64 architecture. The instance is provisioned with 64.0 GB of system memory, yielding a high memory-to-core ratio of 8.0 GB per vCPU. It does not include local storage or bundled GPUs, and comes with zero complimentary public IPv4 addresses.

Performance benchmarks place the n2-highmem-8 consistently in the average tier for most compute and memory operations. Single-core and multi-core stress-ng tests return average performance ratings, supported by a 33 MB L3 cache and AVX-512 instruction set extensions. Memory bandwidth is robust, reaching 413.33 GB/sec for cached small blocks and maintaining 52.02 GB/sec for uncached large block reads. However, multi-threaded bzip3 decompression and complex graphical tasks like ray tracing and image processing fall into the weak performance category.

This instance is designed for memory-intensive workloads that do not require dedicated GPU acceleration or local NVMe storage. It is highly suitable for database operations, caching layers such as Redis, and memory-heavy enterprise applications. The qualitative cost profile is efficient for memory-dense deployments, offering a high RAM-to-vCPU ratio without the premium cost of dedicated storage or accelerator hardware. It is less suited for heavy multi-threaded decompression or large-scale LLM inference, where its performance drops to weak or poor tiers.
description: Google Cloud Platform n2-highmem-8 is a memory-optimized virtual machine featuring 8 vCPUs and 64.0 GB of RAM, powered by an Intel Xeon x86_64 processor running at 2.8 GHz. With 8.0 GB of memory per vCPU, this instance is designed for memory-intensive workloads. It does not include bundled GPUs or local storage. Benchmark results indicate average performance across standard compute, database operations, and memory bandwidth, with cached read speeds reaching 413.33 GB/sec. However, it exhibits weaker performance in multi-threaded decompression and graphical tasks like ray tracing. This instance is qualitatively cost-efficient for applications requiring significant memory capacity without the overhead of local NVMe storage or hardware accelerators. It is best suited for database operations, caching systems like Redis, and general-purpose memory-heavy workloads.
og_description: Google Cloud Platform n2-highmem-8 is a memory-optimized instance with 8 Intel Xeon vCPUs and 64 GB RAM. It offers balanced mid-tier performance for database operations and memory-intensive workloads.
meta_description: Google Cloud Platform n2-highmem-8 is an x86_64 memory-optimized instance with 8 vCPUs, 64 GB RAM, and average database and memory performance.
tagline: A memory-optimized virtual machine featuring a high RAM-to-vCPU ratio for database operations and memory-intensive enterprise applications.
bullet_points: 8 vCPUs and 64.0 GB of RAM providing 8.0 GB per core.
Intel Xeon x86_64 processor running at 2.8 GHz with AVX-512 support.
Average performance in database operations and Redis caching.
No bundled local storage or GPU accelerators.
Best suited for memory-intensive workloads and database deployments.
